functional_purpose: Receives HL7v2 document messages and extracts patient, TXA, and OBX document metadata and binary content. It submits a document_upload_request to the OpenEyes PayloadProcessor queue.
business_process: Inbound clinical document upload for association with an OpenEyes patient record.
ack_behaviour: MLLP auto-generated HL7 ACK before processing: AA on success, AE on error, and AR on rejection.
main_transformer_stages: Extract MR patient identifier from PID
Map document date, reference, filename, subtype, laterality, firm, and binary data from TXA/OBX
Derive MIME type from filename extension
error_handling: The source defines standard HL7 AE/AR ACK responses; the HTTP destination does not validate its response.
queue_behaviour: Source queue buffer is 1000; destination queueing is disabled.
logging: The PatientId extraction script logs an error if mapping fails; this creates a potential identifier-related log risk.

evidence.claim: Optegra | DOCUMENTS IN | 1208a953-4fe1-457a-85b3-f17f56a5b1c1 | Optegra/Default Group.xml: the source connector is a TCP Listener on port 6662 using MLLP and HL7V2.

Optegra | DOCUMENTS IN | 1208a953-4fe1-457a-85b3-f17f56a5b1c1 | Optegra/Default Group.xml: Send Document POSTs document_upload_request parameters and document content to the PayloadProcessor endpoint using preemptive HTTP Basic with a redacted password.
Optegra | DOCUMENTS IN | 1208a953-4fe1-457a-85b3-f17f56a5b1c1 | Optegra/Default Group.xml: its destination transformer maps PID, TXA, and OBX fields and derives MIME type from the filename.

functional_purpose: Polls a local document drop directory and forwards each binary file unchanged to an SFTP directory. Successfully processed source files are deleted.
business_process: Outbound transfer of correspondence or documents to an external document-management file share.
main_transformer_stages: Pass raw binary content through unchanged
Write using the original filename
error_handling: Unreadable source files have no configured action; source response errors are moved to a local error directory.
queue_behaviour: Source queue buffer is 1000; destination queueing is disabled.
logging: No channel-specific logging steps are configured.

evidence.claim: Optegra | Docman | 04524f4d-5201-4c16-b66f-26c3d7783d85 | Optegra/Default Group.xml: File Reader polls /mnt/docman for binary files, deletes them after processing, and moves response errors to /mnt/docman/error.

Optegra | Docman | 04524f4d-5201-4c16-b66f-26c3d7783d85 | Optegra/Default Group.xml: Destination 1 is a File Writer using SFTP password authentication, writes the original filename, and has redacted credentials.

functional_purpose: Reads migration files whose filenames encode document metadata, then creates OpenEyes document records with the file content. It also contains search and update connectors, although the search and update paths are disabled.
business_process: Bulk migration of historical documents into OpenEyes.
main_filters: Accept only files whose original filename has eight double-underscore-separated parts
Enabled Create path is not filtered by the disabled no-search-results rule
main_transformer_stages: Parse encoded filename into patient, firm, subtype, laterality, reference, date, filename, and MIME type
Search for an existing document
Create a document with the raw file content
Disabled update path would PATCH a single search result
error_handling: Search, Create, and Update response transformers record an error message for unexpected HTTP status; only Create is enabled among the write connectors.
retry_behaviour: All destinations have queueing disabled and retryCount 0.
queue_behaviour: Source queue buffer is 1000; destination queueing is disabled.
logging: No explicit logger calls are configured; migration metadata, including patient identifiers and filenames, is stored as message metadata.

evidence.claim: Optegra | Document Migration | 1a5ecbc6-de5e-4bb1-bb23-fad5d2c25488 | Optegra/Default Group.xml: File Reader consumes /mnt/document-upload and transformer stages parse metadata from the filename and derive a MIME type.
Optegra | Document Migration | 1a5ecbc6-de5e-4bb1-bb23-fad5d2c25488 | Optegra/Default Group.xml: the source filter requires eight double-underscore-separated filename parts.

Optegra | Document Migration | 1a5ecbc6-de5e-4bb1-bb23-fad5d2c25488 | Optegra/Default Group.xml: Search uses GET /api/v2/Document/search, Create uses POST /api/v2/Document/create and is enabled, and Update uses PATCH /api/v2/Document/update but is disabled; all use HTTP Basic with a redacted password.
evidence.location: destination Search, Create, and Update properties and filters, lines 241-743

functional_purpose: Receives DICOM objects, extracts DICOM header and AET metadata, writes the original DICOM file to a local directory, and submits it as a dicom_request to PayloadProcessor. The local IOLMaster import destination is removed unless DICOM headers identify a compatible Zeiss IOLMaster object.
business_process: Ingestion of device imaging and biometry output for OpenEyes processing.

destination_connectors.name: Write out file to IolMasterImport incoming folder when device is a Zeiss IOLMaster

ack_behaviour: DICOM listener auto-generates its response after source transformation.
main_filters: Remove the IOLMaster file-write destination unless manufacturer is Zeiss, model contains iolmaster, and SOP class is supported
main_transformer_stages: Extract patient, study, device, SOP class, and AET DICOM tags
Extract identifier type and institution from local AET
Set source platform from AET
Conditionally remove the IOLMaster file-write destination
error_handling: No explicit error transformer is configured; DICOM response is generated after transformation and the HTTP response is not validated.
retry_behaviour: Destination queues are disabled and retryCount is 0.
queue_behaviour: Source queue buffer is 1000; destination queueing is disabled.
logging: No channel-specific logger calls are configured; message storage and attachments are enabled, creating a DICOM patient-data retention risk.

evidence.claim: Optegra | HFA2 | 81492d92-15a9-4109-8b65-9d95aa131200 | Optegra/Dicom.xml: the source is a DICOM Listener on port 11113, and its source transformer maps patient, study, device, SOP class, and AET DICOM tags.

Optegra | HFA2 | 81492d92-15a9-4109-8b65-9d95aa131200 | Optegra/Dicom.xml: the File Writer writes to /mnt/dicom, while PayloadProcessor API Send POSTs a dicom_request with DICOM content to /api/v1/request/queue/add using preemptive HTTP Basic with a redacted password.
evidence.location: destinations File Writer and PayloadProcessor API Send, lines 288-472

evidence.claim: Optegra | HFA2 | 81492d92-15a9-4109-8b65-9d95aa131200 | Optegra/Dicom.xml: the response transformer removes the IOLMaster file destination unless the headers identify a Zeiss IOLMaster and a supported SOP class.

functional_purpose: Receives DICOM objects, extracts AET-derived routing metadata, conditionally writes compatible Zeiss IOLMaster DICOM files to the IOLMasterImport directory, and sends DICOM payloads to PayloadProcessor.
business_process: Import of IOLMaster biometry DICOM output, with general DICOM submission to OpenEyes processing.

destination_connectors.name: Write out file to IolMasterImport incoming folder when device is a Zeiss IOLMaster

ack_behaviour: DICOM listener auto-generates its response after source transformation.
main_filters: Remove IOLMaster file-write destination unless headers show a compatible Zeiss IOLMaster device and supported SOP class
main_transformer_stages: Read local and remote AET
Extract identifier type and institution from local AET
Set source_platform from AET
Extract DICOM headers and remove the IOLMaster file-write destination if incompatible
error_handling: No explicit error transformer is configured; the HTTP response is not validated.
retry_behaviour: Destination queues are disabled and retryCount is 0.
queue_behaviour: Source queue buffer is 1000; destination queueing is disabled.
logging: No channel-specific logger calls are configured; message storage and attachments are enabled, creating a DICOM patient-data retention risk.

evidence.claim: Optegra | IOL | c14efd23-2c1c-4e53-a59f-dcfe0e727c3b | Optegra/Dicom.xml: the source is a DICOM Listener on port 11112 and its enabled source stages derive identifier, institution, and source platform from AET metadata.

Optegra | IOL | c14efd23-2c1c-4e53-a59f-dcfe0e727c3b | Optegra/Dicom.xml: getDicomHeaders reads raw DICOM, extracts headers, and removes destination ID 1 unless the device is Zeiss IOLMaster with a supported SOP class.
evidence.location: destination getDicomHeaders and response transformer, lines 298-396

evidence.claim: Optegra | IOL | c14efd23-2c1c-4e53-a59f-dcfe0e727c3b | Optegra/Dicom.xml: destination ID 1 writes a DICOM file to /mnt/dicom and PayloadProcessor API Send POSTs dicom_request content to /api/v1/request/queue/add using preemptive HTTP Basic with a redacted password.
evidence.location: destinations File Writer and PayloadProcessor API Send, lines 401-625

functional_purpose: Receives HL7v2 PAS events and maps them to PASAPI V2 Patient, PatientAppointment, and PatientMerge requests. Destination filters route A40 merges, A38 deletion, and appointment lifecycle events to the applicable PASAPI resources.
business_process: Synchronisation of PAS patient demographics, patient merges, and appointment status with OpenEyes.
ack_behaviour: MLLP auto-generated HL7 ACK before processing: AA on success, AE on error, and AR on rejection.
main_filters: Secondary Patient and Patient Merge accept only A40
PatientAppointment PUT accepts A01, A05, A08, A11, A03, A13, A02, and A12 except A08 with PV1.4 CANCL
main_transformer_stages: Transform HL7 demographics to PASAPI Patient XML
Extract patient and visit identifiers and appointment details from PID, PV1, PV2, EVN, and MRG
Derive appointment status
Build PatientAppointment and PatientMerge XML payloads
Inspect PASAPI responses for warnings and patient identifiers
error_handling: Response transformers inspect PASAPI warning comments and log warning or error content; destination response validation is disabled.
retry_behaviour: All destinations have queueing disabled and retryCount 0.
queue_behaviour: Source queue buffer is 1000; destination queueing is disabled.
logging: Transformer and response scripts log mapping exceptions and PASAPI warnings/errors, which can include patient-related operational data.

evidence.claim: Optegra | PAS IN V2 | d69815ee-68d1-4dcb-8896-8b12ba06a9c4 | Optegra/PAS.xml: the source is a TCP Listener on port 6661 using MLLP and HL7V2.
Optegra | PAS IN V2 | d69815ee-68d1-4dcb-8896-8b12ba06a9c4 | Optegra/PAS.xml: PASAPI - Secondary Patient PUTs V2 Patient data, maps PID/PV1/PV2/EVN/MRG fields, and accepts only A40.
evidence.location: destination PASAPI - Secondary Patient properties, transformer, and filter, lines 148-617

evidence.claim: Optegra | PAS IN V2 | d69815ee-68d1-4dcb-8896-8b12ba06a9c4 | Optegra/PAS.xml: Clinic List PUT sends PatientAppointment for A01, A05, A08, A11, A03, A13, A02, and A12; Clinic List DELETE handles A38; Patient Merge PUTs V2 PatientMerge for A40.
evidence.location: destination Clinic List PUT, Clinic List DELETE, and PASAPI - Patient Merge properties and filters, lines 1182-1953

evidence.claim: Optegra | PAS IN V2 | d69815ee-68d1-4dcb-8896-8b12ba06a9c4 | Optegra/PAS.xml: all HTTP destinations use preemptive HTTP Basic as api with a redacted password and have queueing disabled with retryCount 0.
